content:
Key Findings

  • South Ribble Borough Council paid 20 suppliers over £1 million each between 2021/22 and 2025/26
  • The top supplier, LANCS COUNTY COUNCIL, received £23.1 million
  • The total spent on these 20 suppliers was £120.5 million
  • CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD was the largest private sector recipient with £17.6 million
  • The council transacted with 1,850 unique suppliers during this period

The Million-Pound Club

South Ribble Borough Council paid 20 suppliers over £1 million each between 2021/22 and 2025/26. These transactions accounted for £120.5 million of the council's total spending of £178.0 million. The largest recipient was LANCS COUNTY COUNCIL, which received £23.1 million. This substantial payment highlights the interdependence between local authorities in delivering public services.

The second largest payment went to CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD, a private sector construction firm, which received £17.6 million. This significant investment in construction services suggests ongoing infrastructure projects or property developments within the borough. The third largest recipient was the DEPARTMENT FOR COMMUNITIES AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT, which received £15.1 million, indicating substantial central government funding or shared service agreements.

Top Suppliers by Sector

The top suppliers to South Ribble Borough Council span various sectors, including local government, construction, and leisure services. The largest private sector recipient was CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD with £17.6 million, followed by RP TYSON CONSTRUCTION LTD with £5.2 million and JOHN TURNER CONSTRUCTION GROUP LTD with £2.5 million. These payments reflect the council's investment in infrastructure and development projects.

Leisure services also featured prominently, with ALLIANCE LEISURE SERVICES LTD receiving £8.8 million and SOUTH RIBBLE LEISURE LTD receiving £4.6 million. These payments highlight the council's commitment to maintaining and improving leisure facilities for residents. Additionally, KOMPAN LTD, a supplier of playground equipment, received £2.0 million, further emphasising the council's focus on community amenities.

Comparing the Top Suppliers

The following table lists the top 10 suppliers to South Ribble Borough Council by total spending:

Supplier Amount (£)
LANCS COUNTY COUNCIL 23,100,000
CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD 17,600,000
DEPARTMENT FOR COMMUNITIES AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT 15,100,000
CHORLEY BOROUGH COUNCIL 14,100,000
ALLIANCE LEISURE SERVICES LTD 8,800,000
RP TYSON CONSTRUCTION LTD 5,200,000
INLAND REVENUE 0065PV00107808 5,000,000
DEPT FOR BUSINESS ENERGY & IND. STRATEGY (BEIS) 4,900,000
SOUTH RIBBLE LEISURE LTD 4,600,000
HM REVENUE & CUSTOMS 065PV00107808 4,200,000

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to South Ribble Borough Council about the specific projects involving CADDICK CONSTRUCTION (NW) LTD and RP TYSON CONSTRUCTION LTD
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about ongoing infrastructure and leisure service projects
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the full details of these transactions
  • Write to your local councillor about the council's spending on construction and leisure services
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse other suppliers
This article is based on South Ribble Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
